content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

July 12, 1990

Alan J. Gardner  
California Cable Television Association  
4341 Piedmont Avenue  
Oakland, CA 94611

Re: Your Request for Advice  
Our File No. A-90-414

Dear Mr. Gardner:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided under the lobbying provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹

Your letter concerns what constitutes a lobbying contact for an individual when attempting to influence the administrative actions of the California Public Utilities Commission at a workshop held under circumstances consistent with Regulation 18239(d)(1). Your letter accurately reflects that advice.

When a meeting of the CPUC is held in accordance with Regulation 18239(d)(1), attempts to influence the actions of the CPUC at that meeting come within the definition of "administrative testimony," which is not required to be counted for purposes of determining whether an individual qualifies as a "lobbyist" under Section 82039 and Regulation 18239(a)-(c). However, if any of the conditions outlined in Regulation 18239(d)(1) are not met, such attempts to influence would be counted.

A copy of your letter is enclosed for your convenience.

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

Alan J. Gardner
Page Two

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Kevin S. Braaten-Moen
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure
